summary: Small multi-file decomp/matching cleanup that closed many near-matching functions, mostly in Kirby special-move code, with additional fighter, game-mode, stage, item, toy, and sysdolphin/baselib touches. The bot report claims 52 new matches and +12392 matched-code bytes; the diff shows a mix of stack padding adjustments, typed field recovery, signature/header synchronization, enum/constant cleanup, and removal of temporary padding or dummy locals.

what_changed: Touched 24 files with 97 added and 80 deleted lines, concentrated in Kirby special-move source plus smaller game-mode, stage, item, toy, and baselib changes.
Bot report for GALE01 917c487..99d50d4 records matched code increasing to 55.12%, +0.32%, +12392 bytes, with 52 new matches and one unmatched-item improvement.
Kirby special-N family changes include stack padding reshuffles, local order changes, cleanup of dummy locals, enum/constant substitution, field access corrections, and small control/data-flow fixes.
Game mode code recovered a typed TmData x524 HSD_Text* array and used it in fn_80198C60 instead of pointer arithmetic through (u8*) td + 0x524.
gm_801BEFA4 signature changed from s8 to int in both implementation and header.
Several functions removed padding locals, while others added or resized PAD_STACK to close matching gaps.
smart_moves: Updated both gm_1BA8.c and gm_1BA8.h together when changing gm_801BEFA4's parameter type, matching the truthful-header pattern.
Replaced raw offset-array access in gm_18A5.c with a real TmData field and updated gm/types.h accordingly, reducing pointer arithmetic while preserving layout intent.
Used named enum/motion-state constants such as ftKb_MS_FeSpecialAirNLoop instead of routing a magic value through a temporary integer in ftKb_SpecialNYs.c.
Cleaned obvious generated temporaries and redundant assignments where the diff shows equivalent direct forms, such as fp2->fv.kb.xC0 = NULL and direct cmd_vars[3] indexing.
Kept stack tactics narrow at function scope where PAD_STACK changes were used, rather than broad file-level pragmas.

decomp_lessons.lesson: When a recovered struct field explains repeated offset-based array access, pair the body cleanup with the owning type definition update.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: gm_18A5.c replaces arr = (HSD_Text**) ((u8*) td + 0x524) with td->x524[2]/[3]
diff_excerpt: gm/types.h replaces pad_x524 with HSD_Text* x524[4]

decomp_lessons.lesson: Header prototypes should be synchronized in the same change when a function signature is recovered or adjusted.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: gm_801BEFA4 parameter s8 -> int in gm_1BA8.c
diff_excerpt: gm_801BEFA4 prototype s8 -> int in gm_1BA8.h

decomp_lessons.lesson: Small matching closures may depend on local declaration order and exact explicit casts, but those changes should remain tied to concrete functions rather than treated as general style.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: ftKb_SpecialN_800F5D04 reorders Vec3 vel/pos and adds PAD_STACK(8)
text_corpus: bot report lists both functions as new 100% matches or included among new matches

naming_conventions.convention: Prefer known named constants/enums over temporary variables holding magic values when the target motion state is known.
examples: ftKb_MS_FeSpecialAirNLoop replaces unsigned long long new_var = 0x217; msid = new_var

naming_conventions.convention: Use local role names like fp2 for a second Fighter* loaded from gobj->user_data when that access shape is needed.
examples: Fighter* fp2 = gobj->user_data; fp2->pre_hitlag_cb = NULL; fp2->post_hitlag_cb = NULL
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: repeated callback clearing blocks in ftKb_SpecialNNs.c

naming_conventions.convention: New struct field names in this slice remain offset-style when semantics are not proven, as with TmData::x524.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Narrow PAD_STACK insertion/removal/resizing was used across near-matching functions to alter stack layout.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: PAD_STACK additions in ftKb_SpecialNPk.c and ftKb_SpecialNZd.c
diff_excerpt: PAD_STACK removals in ftKb_SpecialAirS_Coll, ftKb_EatTurn_Anim, ftKb_SpecialNMr_800F93CC
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caution: Current standards treat PAD_STACK as an evidence-requiring tactic. The bot report supports match outcomes for many functions, but the slice does not include per-function objdiff output for every PAD_STACK edit.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Declaration order and temporary lifetime were adjusted to affect stack/register allocation.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: Vec3 vel before Vec3 pos in ftKb_SpecialN_800F5D04
diff_excerpt: local scale_y block in ftCo_800D105C
diff_excerpt: scoped fp2 block in ftKb_SpecialNNs.c callback clearing
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caution: These are matching tactics, not broad style guidance.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Typed fields replaced raw pointer arithmetic once TmData layout was represented in the header.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: TmData HSD_Text* x524[4] in gm/types.h
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caution: This aligns with typed-fields-over-pointer-math, but the exact field name x524 remains offset-style.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Explicit casts and exact comparison forms were used where they likely affected MWCC codegen.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caution: The slice shows the edits and global bot progress, not detailed compiler reasoning.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Recover typed struct fields when raw offset access repeats
accepted_candidate_records.text: In PR 2219, fn_80198C60 changed from casting TmData + 0x524 to HSD_Text** into using td->x524[2]/[3], and gm/types.h replaced pad_x524 with HSD_Text* x524[4]. This is a concrete example of moving from pointer arithmetic to typed fields when the layout is supported.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: gm_18A5.c fn_80198C60 typed td->x524 access

accepted_candidate_records.title: Synchronize headers with recovered signatures
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR 2219 changed gm_801BEFA4's parameter from s8 to int in both gm_1BA8.c and gm_1BA8.h, showing a small signature recovery paired with its owning header update.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Prefer named motion-state constants over magic-value temporaries when known
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR 2219 replaced an unsigned long long temporary holding 0x217 with ftKb_MS_FeSpecialAirNLoop before assigning msid in ftKb_SpecialNMs_8010BB50.

source_update_candidates.title: Document PR 2219 as an example, not a blanket exception, for PAD_STACK matching tactics
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2219 contains many narrow PAD_STACK additions, removals, and size changes associated with reported new matches. It can serve as an example that stack padding may be used surgically for matching, but should not weaken the existing requirement for explicit verifier evidence and cleanup consideration.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: PAD_STACK changes across ftKb_SpecialN.c, ftKb_SpecialNNs.c, ftKb_SpecialNPk.c, gm_18A5.c, grfourside.c
text_corpus: bot report says 52 new matches
source_update_candidates.reason: The slice supports a standards example/case study, but not a new accepted global rule permitting PAD_STACK without narrow evidence.

source_update_candidates.title: Kirby special-N files frequently require stack-shape/local-order tuning for final matching

evidence_refs: text_corpus: bot report lists numerous Kirby functions as new 100% matches
source_update_candidates.reason: Useful path-local matching note, but should be curated carefully because the PR lacks author explanation and full diff is truncated.

source_update_candidates.title: TmData offset 0x524 is represented as HSD_Text* x524[4] after PR 2219
source_update_candidates.text: The PR replaces TmData padding at 0x524 with HSD_Text* x524[4] and uses td->x524[2]/[3] in fn_80198C60.
source_update_candidates.reason: Potential source-owned path fact about struct layout; curator should verify against current source before promoting.

rejection_notes: Do not infer that every touched function was matched solely because of the visible diff; the bot report is truncated after listing a subset and the raw diff is truncated.
Do not infer human reviewer approval rationale; there are no human review comments in the loaded slice.
Do not promote PAD_STACK as normal style from this PR; standards still require explicit verifier evidence and narrow scope.
Do not infer semantics for offset-style fields such as TmData::x524 beyond the HSD_Text* array type shown in the diff.
